title: Secretome of Solanum tuberosum grown in the presence of proalexin
projectDescription: Secretome of Desiree potato grown in the presence of phosphite (proalexin).
sampleProcessingProtocol: Secretome isolation was performed using vacuum infiltration with phosphate buffer saline. Samples were run on SDS PAGE. after coomassie staining the entire lane was subjecteed to in/gel digestion
dataProcessingProtocol: Files were converted to mzML and MGF using Proteowizard. MS&MS searches were perfromed using the MGF files in X!Tandem and Mascot. Precursor tolerance 5 ppm and fragment tolerance 0.5 Da with one missed cleavage allowed. Database was all UniProt Solanum proteins and proteins from the Potato Genome project as of 2011/08/24.

references.referenceLine: Burra D, Berkowitz O, Hedley PE, Morris J, Resjö S, Levander F, Liljeroth E, Andreasson E, Alexandersson E. Phosphite-induced changes of the transcriptome and secretome in Solanum tuberosum leading to resistance against Phytophthora infestans. BMC Plant Biol. 2014 Oct 1;14(1):254

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset
